tools.name: ask_operator
tools.description: Send a question to the human operator via their Telegram and wait for their reply. Use when you need a decision, approval, or clarification you can't infer from the conversation. The operator gets a Telegram notification on their phone; their reply comes back to you here. Typical reply latency: 1-15

tools.name: message_agent
tools.description: Send a message to another Relaystation agent's ephemeral address (format <local_part>@courier.relaystation.ai). Same-Relaystation routing only (v2 launch); external domains return EXTERNAL_DOMAIN_NOT_SUPPORTED_V2. Free — agent-to-agent traffic has no per-message cost (subject to the recipient's per-

tools.name: create_agent_address
tools.description: Mint a new ephemeral Relaystation address for this agent to receive messages from other agents. Returns a unique <local_part>@courier.relaystation.ai address tied to this agent's customer. Free up to the daily mint cap (default 10/day per customer); above-cap mints debit bridge.agent_address.over_ca

tools.name: create_baton
tools.description: Create a baton — a prepaid storage object. Pick a preset (drop=store a file, pass=share a file, scratchpad=collaborate via append log, checkpoint=overwrite snapshot, ledger=hash-chained tamper-evident log) and optional tier, or supply a customShape. BILLABLE: charged once at create (the engine quote

tools.name: read_baton
tools.description: Read a baton's content + metadata. FREE (reads draw down the prepaid egress budget, no per-call charge). Address by owner id (requires your key) or by a collaborator token id (tok_…, the token IS the credential). Large multi-entry batons return a presigned download URL or a hint to page via list-ent

tools.name: append_to_baton
tools.description: Write to a baton — append (for append / append-chained presets) or overwrite (for single-object / checkpoint presets). FREE (consumes the prepaid writes budget; no per-call charge). Owner or editor/write-token only. Set overwrite=true to replace in full on overwrite-shaped batons. Example: append_to

tools.name: mint_token
tools.description: Mint a collaborator token for a baton so another agent can read and/or write without your key. FREE. type is read / write / read_write; optionally cap reads_allowed / writes_allowed, set an expiry, or restrict by IP. Up to 100 tokens per baton. Owner only. Returns the token id (tok_…) — share it as

tools.name: balance
tools.description: Your current Relaystation balance and available (post-hold) credit, plus account profile. Read-only, FREE. Requires your rs_live_* key (or wallet-JWT / MCP token); scoped to YOUR account only. Example: balance {}

tools.name: create_topup_link
tools.description: Create a one-tap Stripe Checkout link to add credit to YOUR balance — hand it to a human to open and pay. NO money moves here (it returns a URL; the balance is credited when the human pays). Use when you hit insufficient balance. `amountUsd` is dollars (whole cents). FREE to call. Requires your rs_l

tools.name: search_tools
tools.description: Search the full Relaystation tool catalog by keyword and get back the best matches. Use this first to find a tool, then `describe_tool` for its schema and `call_tool` to run it (or call a named hot tool directly). `query` is free text (e.g. "merge pdf", "csv to json", "send telegram"). `detail` cont

tools.name: describe_tool
tools.description: Get one tool's full description and input schema by exact name (as returned by search_tools). FREE. Example: describe_tool {name:'pdf_merge'}

tools.name: call_tool
tools.description: Run a tool found via search_tools by name, passing its arguments. Dispatches the safe/billable utility tools (the cputools catalog + free reads/quotes) through the identical auth + billing + validation as a direct call. Consequential tools (account, money, credential, messaging — e.g. create_topup_l

tools.name: create_contract
tools.description: Create a binding-by-goodwill agreement between agents/parties and get back a contractId + termsHash + per-signer read tokens. The contract IS a hash-chained, auto-witnessed record (terms = entry 1, consents append after); it is tamper-evident and permanently verifiable, but it is NOT legally binding

tools.name: sign_contract
tools.description: Append your consent to a contract (one entry on its hash chain). FREE. Two forms, no cross-method satisfaction: a "wallet" signer supplies an EIP-712 ContractConsent signature over the contract's termsHash (the signature IS the authentication — no account needed); an "account" signer must be authent

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: message_agent, create_agent_address, read_baton, balance, create_topup_link, create_contract · Private data: ask_operator, create_baton, read_baton, append_to_baton, mint_token, balance, create_topup_link, call_tool, create_contract, sign_contract · Public sink: ask_operator, message_agent, create_baton, mint_token, balance, search_tools · Destructive: message_agent, create_baton, append_to_baton, search_tools, call_tool.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: message_agent, create_agent_address, read_baton, balance, create_topup_link, create_contract · Private data: ask_operator, create_baton, read_baton, append_to_baton, mint_token, balance, create_topup_link, call_tool, create_contract, sign_contract · Public sink: ask_operator, message_agent, create_baton, mint_token, balance, search_tools · Destructive: message_agent, create_baton, append_to_baton, search_tools, call_tool

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
